reasoning: Paul Biedermann has held the 200m freestyle long course world record (1:42.00) since 2009, set during the supersuit era. Contemporary elite swimmers are typically 1.5 to 3 seconds away from this time; the closest anyone has come recently is David Popovici's 1:42.97 in 2022, and the 2024 Olympic final was won in 1:44.72. The late fall/winter months (November to January) also coincide primarily with the short course (25m pool) season, making record-breaking taper attempts in long course extremely unlikely in this narrow window up to January 2026. Therefore, the probability of the record remaining intact is exceptionally high, though I reduce it slightly for the later dates to account for general uncertainty and long-term variability.
